name: All Saints
description_short: All Saints Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative offering savings accounts, checking, loans, and credit cards with competitive rates and a community-focused mission.
description_long:
All Saints Federal Credit Union operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative guided by the principle of 'People Helping People.' Like all credit unions, it pools resources from members to provide affordable financial services while returning earnings to members in the form of better rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks. The organization is federally chartered and NCUA-insured, making it a legitimate alternative to commercial banking institutions.

All Saints FCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share savings accounts, share certificates (CDs), share draft checking accounts, debit cards, personal loans, auto loans, and credit cards. They also provide ancillary services such as online bill pay, direct deposit, home banking with account alerts, notary services, and student savings accounts. Auto loan rates are prominently featured, with new vehicle financing as low as 3.99% and used vehicles (2022-2025) as low as 4.99%. Money market accounts offer rates as high as 2.25%, and credit cards start at 10.99% APR.

What distinguishes All Saints FCU is its explicit credit union structure where each member is a full owner with equal voting rights, meaning earnings are returned to members rather than distributed to shareholders. The organization emphasizes member service over profit maximization and markets itself as an alternative for those seeking affordable credit and wealth-building opportunities. They actively promote membership and highlight their community connection and commitment to member interests in all messaging.

Based on available website content, All Saints FCU is a legitimate, established credit union with standard offerings comparable to other credit unions. The main limitation is that specific information about membership eligibility, location, asset size, and full terms is not detailed on the reviewed pages. Their website provides basic service descriptions but lacks detailed fee schedules, membership requirements, or expanded service information that would be typical for a comprehensive profile.
services: Personal Loans with fixed rates and no pre-payment fees
pros: Competitive auto loan rates as low as 3.99% for new vehicles and 4.99% for used vehicles (2022-2025)
No pre-payment fees on loans with fixed interest rates across all loan products
Member-owned structure means earnings are returned to members as better rates rather than going to shareholders
Offers high-yield money market savings accounts at 2.25% APR
Full-service financial institution with checking, savings, credit cards, and loans under one roof
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection for members
Online banking platform with home banking, bill pay, and account alerts via email or text
Equal member voting rights—each member has one vote regardless of account balance
cons: Website does not clearly specify membership eligibility or location-based restrictions
Limited information about credit card rewards or benefits compared to competitors
No details provided on monthly fees for checking accounts or other account maintenance costs
Loan pre-qualification or rate-shopping tools not visible on the website
Physical branch locations and ATM network details are not comprehensively listed
best_for: Members seeking competitive auto loan rates with no pre-payment penalties
Consumers looking for member-owned financial institutions that prioritize member benefits over profits
Savers interested in competitive money market and certificate rates
Individuals seeking a full-service credit union with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards
diagnosis: All Saints FCU is best for members seeking a member-owned alternative to traditional banks with competitive loan rates, particularly for auto financing, and who value community-focused financial services. The main caveat is that specific membership eligibility requirements, physical locations, and detailed fee structures are not clearly documented on their website, so prospective members should contact them directly at (216) 524-0414 to confirm eligibility and review complete terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: All Saints — Credit Union in Parma, OH | CreditDoc
meta_description: All Saints — NCUA-insured credit union in Parma, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
